summary: Both PFE and XOM are held on the sidelines for this swing cycle due to unresolved binary events that make directional risk too high to enter new positions. PFE is deeply oversold with RSI-7 at 17, creating a tempting mean-reversion setup, but a May 5 earnings report introduces a binary outcome that could easily overwhelm any technical bounce. XOM has a bullish Iran oil supply catalyst in its favor, but the stock is overbought at RSI-7 79.95 and sitting at Fibonacci resistance just as an earnings blackout period begins May 1 — a poor risk/reward moment to chase. In both cases the prudent swing trade discipline is to wait for the event to clear and let price re-establish a clean setup.

PFE.one_liner: Deeply oversold but earnings on May 5 make a bounce bet too dangerous to take.
PFE.key_reason: RSI-7 at 17 signals extreme oversold conditions and a mean-reversion bounce is plausible, but the May 5 earnings report creates a binary risk event that could gap the stock sharply in either direction, invalidating any technical trade thesis before it can play out.
PFE.risk: Entering before earnings risks a large adverse gap; waiting risks missing the bounce if the stock rips on results.

XOM.one_liner: Bullish oil catalyst is real, but overbought technicals at key resistance plus earnings blackout argue for patience.
XOM.key_reason: Iran supply disruption fears provide a genuine fundamental tailwind, but XOM's RSI-7 has reached 79.95 and price is pressing Fibonacci resistance exactly as the pre-earnings quiet period begins May 1, limiting upside and increasing reversal risk.
XOM.risk: A de-escalation in Iran tensions or any earnings guidance miss could trigger a sharp pullback from current extended levels.
